title: Equivalent Units — FIFO
event: Reyes Co.'s beginning WIP was 5,000 units, 60% complete for conversion. During July, 35,000 units were started and completed, and ending WIP is 10,000 units, 40% complete for conversion. Compute FIFO equivalent units for conversion.

computationPaths.narration: FIFO counts only THIS period's work: finish the beginning inventory (the remaining 40%), run the started-and-completed units, start the ending inventory.

memorize.body: FIFO EU = beg WIP × (1 − % already done) + started & completed + end WIP × % done.
FIFO EU is always ≤ weighted-average EU — the difference is exactly the work already inside beginning WIP (here 3,000).

questions.prompt: Using the facts above, FIFO equivalent units for conversion = ?

distractors.feedback: That's weighted average — you counted the 3,000 EU of work done LAST period inside beginning WIP. FIFO strips it out.
You took completed units only. FIFO needs the ending WIP's 4,000 EU of started work — and only 2,000 (not 5,000) for beginning WIP.
